arabic: تَفُتُّ فُؤَادَكَ الأَيَّامُ فَتَّا  * وَتَنْحِتُ جِسْمَكَ السَّاعَاتُ نَحْتًا
english: The days crush your heart with constant crushing. and the hours chisel your body with chiselling.

arabic: وَتَدْعُوكَ الْمَنُونُ دُعَاءَ صِدْقٍ  * أَلَا يَا صَاحِ أَنتَ أُرِيدُ أَنتَا
english: And death calls you with a truthful call: “Oh companion, it is you I want, it is you.”

arabic: أَرَاكَ تُحِبُّ عِرْسًا ذَاتَ خِدْرٍ  * أَبَتَّ طَلَاقَهَا الأَكْيَاسُ بَتًّا
english: I see you love a bride who is veiled and secluded, yet the wise have firmly decided to divorce her.

arabic: تَنَامُ الدَّهْرَ وَيْحَكَ فِي غَطِيطٍ  * بِهَا حَتَّى إِذَا مِتَّ انْتَبَهْتَا
english: You sleep your whole life — woe to you! — in deep snoring, until when you die, then you finally wake up.

arabic: فَكَمْ ذَا أَنْتَ مَخْدُوعٌ وَحَتَّى  * مَتَى لَا تَرْعَوِي عَنْهَا وَحَتَّى
english: So how long will you go on being deceived? and until when will you not abstain from it? Until when?

arabic: أَبا بَكْرٍ دَعَوْتُكَ لَوْ أَجَبْتَ  * إِلَى مَا فِيهِ حَظُّكَ لَوْ عَقَلْتَا
english: Oh Abu Bakr, I called you, if only you had responded, to that which contains your benefit, if you had used your reason.

arabic: إِلَى عِلْمٍ تَكُونُ بِهِ إِمَامًا  * مُطَاعًا إِنْ نَهَيْتَ وَإِنْ أَمَرْتَا
english: To knowledge through which you would become an Imam, obeyed when you forbid, and when you command.

arabic: وَيَجْلُو مَا بِعَيْنِكَ مِنْ غِشَاهَا  * وَيَهْدِيكَ السَّبِيلَ إِذَا ضَلَلْتَا
english: And it clears away what is covering your eye, and it guides you to the path when you go astray.

arabic: وَتَحْمِلُ مِنهُ فِي نَادِيكَ تَاجًا  * وَيَكْسُوكَ الجَمَالَ إِذَا عَرِيتَا
english: And from it, you carry a crown in your gathering, and it clothes you with beauty when you are bare.

arabic: يَنَالُكَ نَفْعُهُ مَا دُمْتَ حَيًّا  * وَيَبْقَى ذِكْرُهُ لَكَ إِنْ ذَهَبْتَا
english: Its benefit reaches you as long as you are alive, and its mention (your reputation through it) remains for you when you are gone.

arabic: هُوَ الْعَضْبُ الْمُهَنَّدُ لَيْسَ يَنْبُو  * تُصِيبُ بِهِ مَقَاتِلَ مَنْ أَرَدتَّا
english: It is a sharp, Indian-forged sword that does not miss its mark, you strike with it the vital points of whomever you wish.

arabic: وَكَنْزٌ لَا تَخَافُ عَلَيْهِ لِصًّا  * خَفِيفُ الحَمْلِ يُوجَدُ حَيْثُ كُنْتَا
english: And it is a treasure you do not fear thieves for, Light to carry, and found wherever you are.

arabic: يَزِيدُ بِكَثْرَةِ الإِنْفَاقِ مِنْهُ  * وَينقُصُ إِنْ بِهِ كَفًّا شَدَدْتَا
english: It increases the more you spend from it, and it decreases if you tighten your hand over it.

arabic: فَلَوْ قَدْ ذُقْتَ مِنْ حَلْوَاهُ طَعْمًا  * لَآثَرْتَ التَّعَلُّمَ وَاجْتَهَدْتَا
english: If you had tasted even a bit of its sweetness, you would have preferred learning and strived hard for it.

arabic: وَلَمْ يَشْغَلْكَ عَنْهُ هَوًى مُطَاعٌ  * وَلَا دُنْيَا بِزُخْرُفِهَا فُتِنْتَا
english: And no obeyed desire would have distracted you from it, nor the worldly life with its glitter would have tempted you.

arabic: وَلَا أَلْهَاكَ عَنْهُ أَنِيقُ رَوْضٍ  * وَلَا خِدْرٍ بِزِينَتِهَا كَلِفْتَا
english: Nor would an elegant garden have distracted you from it, nor a veiled woman, with her adornment, would you have become infatuated with.

arabic: فَقُوتُ الرُّوحِ أَرْوَاحُ الْمَعَانِي  * وَلَيْسَ بِأَنْ طَعِمْتَ وَلَا شَرِبْتَا
english: For the nourishment of the soul is the spirits of meanings, not that you eat or drink.

arabic: فَوَاظِبْهُ وَخُذْ بِالْجِدِّ فِيهِ  * فَإِنْ أَعْطَاكَهُ اللَّهُ انْتَفَعْتَا
english: So stay committed to it, and pursue it seriously, for if Allah grants it to you, you will benefit from it.

arabic: وَإِنْ أُعْطِيتَ فِيهِ طَوِيلَ بَاعٍ  * وَقالَ النَّاسُ إِنَّكَ قَدْ عَلِمْتَا
english: And if you are given a long reach in it (i.e. deep knowledge), and people say, “Indeed, you have acquired knowledge,”

arabic: فَلَا تَأْمَنْ سُؤَالَ اللَّهِ عَنْهُ  * بِتَوْبِيخٍ عَلِمْتَ فَهَلْ عَمِلْتَا
english: Then do not feel safe from Allah questioning you about it, with reproach: “You knew — so did you act upon it?”

arabic: فَرَأْسُ العِلْمِ تَقْوَى اللَّهِ حَقًّا  * وَلَيْسَ بِأَنْ يُقَالَ لَقَدْ رَأَسْتَا
english: For the pinnacle of knowledge is truly the fear of Allah, not that it is said: "You have become a leader.”

arabic: وَأَفْضَلُ ثَوْبِكَ الْإِحْسَانُ لَكِنْ  * نَرَى ثَوْبَ الإِسَاءَةِ قَدْ لَبِسْتَا
english: And the best of your garments is righteousness (good conduct), but we see that you have worn the garment of wrongdoing.

arabic: إِذَا مَا لَمْ يُفِدْكَ الْعِلْمُ خَيْرًا  * فَخَيْرٌ مِنهُ أَنْ لَوْ قَدْ جَهِلْتَا
english: If knowledge does not bring you benefit, then it would have been better if you had remained ignorant.

arabic: وَإِنْ أَلْقَاكَ فَهْمُكَ فِي مَهَاوٍ  * فَلَيْتَكَ ثُمَّ لَيْتَكَ مَا فَهِمْتَا
english: And if your understanding throws you into [dangerous] pits, then if only you had never understood.

arabic: سَتَجْنِي مِن ثِمَارِ الْعَجْزِ جَهْلًا  * وَتَصْغُرُ فِي الْعُيُونِ إِذَا كَبُرْتَا
english: You will reap ignorance as the fruit of laziness, and you will appear small in the eyes, even when you grow old.

arabic: وَتُفْقَدُ إِنْ جَهِلْتَ وَأَنْتَ بَاقٍ  * وَتُوجَدُ إِنْ عَلِمْتَ وَلَوْ فُقِدتَّا
english: You are lost (forgotten) if you are ignorant, even while you are still alive, and you will be present (remembered) if you have knowledge, even if you are gone.

arabic: وَتَذْكُرُ قَوْلَتِي لَكَ بَعْدَ حِينٍ  * إِذَا حَقًّا بِهَا يَوْمًا عَمِلْتَا
english: And you will remember my words to you after some time, when, one day, you truly act upon them.

arabic: وَإِنْ أَهْمَلْتَهَا وَنَبَذْتَ نُصْحًا  * وَمِلْتَ إِلَى حُطَامٍ قَدْ جَمَعْتَا
english: But if you neglect it and throw away the advice, and incline toward the rubble (worthless things) you have gathered,

arabic: فَسَوفَ تَعَضُّ مِن نَدَمٍ عَلَيْهَا  * وَمَا تُغْنِي النَّدَامَةُ إِن نَدِمْتَا
english: Then you will bite (your hands) in regret over it, and regret will be of no use to you when you regret.

arabic: إِذَا أَبْصَرْتَ صَحْبَكَ فِي سَمَاءٍ  * قَدِ ارْتَفَعُوا عَلَيْكَ وَقَدْ سَفُلْتَا
english: When you see your companions in heaven (i.e., elevated), that they have risen above you, while you have fallen low.

arabic: فَرَاجِعْهَا وَدَعْ عَنْكَ الْهُوَيْنَى  * فَمَا بِالبُطْءِ تُدْرِكُ مَا طَلَبْتَا
english: So return to it (knowledge), and leave off your slowness, for you will not attain what you seek through sluggishness.

arabic: وَلَا تَخْتَلْ بِمَالِكَ وَالْهَ عَنْهُ  * فَلَيْسَ الْمَالُ إِلَّا مَا عَلِمْتَا
english: And do not become conceited with your wealth, and turn away from it, for wealth is nothing but what you have known.

arabic: وَلَيْسَ لِجَاهِلٍ فِي النَّاسِ مُغْنٍ  * وَلَوْ مُلْكُ الْعِرَاقِ لَهُ تَأَتَّى
english: There is no one among people who can satisfy an ignorant person, even if the kingdom of Iraq were made accessible to him.

arabic: سَيَنْطِقُ عَنْكَ عِلْمُكَ فِي مَلَاءٍ  * وَيُكْتَبُ عَنْكَ يَوْمًا إِنْ كَتَبْتَا
english: Your knowledge will speak on your behalf in public, and one day it will be written about you, if you have written.

arabic: وَمَا يُغْنِيْكَ تَشْيِيدُ الْمَبَانِي  * إِذَا بِالجَهْلِ نَفْسَكَ قَد هَدَمْتَا
english: And what benefit is there in building lofty structures, if, through ignorance, you have destroyed your own self?

arabic: جَعَلْتَ الْمَالَ فَوْقَ الْعِلْمِ جَهْلًا  * لَعَمْرُكَ فِي الْقَضِيَّةِ مَا عَدَلْتَا
english: You placed wealth above knowledge — out of ignorance, by your life, in this matter, you have not been just.

arabic: وَبَيْنَهُمَا بِنَصِّ الْوَحْيِ بَوْنٌ  * سَتَعْلَمُهُ إِذَا طَهَ قَرَأْتَا
english: And between them (i.e., knowledge and wealth) is a clear difference by the text of revelation, you will know it when you read [Surah] Taha.

arabic: لَئِنْ رَفَعَ الْغَنِيُّ لِوَاءَ مَالٍ  * لَأَنْتَ لِواءُ عِلْمِكَ قَد رَفَعْتَا
english: If the wealthy one raises the banner of wealth, then you have raised the banner of your knowledge.

arabic: لَئِنْ جَلَسَ الْغَنِيُّ عَلَى الْحَشَايَا  * لَأَنْتَ عَلَى الكَواكِبِ قَدْ جَلَسْتَا
english: If the wealthy sits upon cushions, then you have sat upon the stars.

arabic: وَإِنْ رَكِبَ الْجِيَادَ مُسَوَّمَاتٍ  * لَأَنْتَ مَناهِجَ التَّقْوَى رَكِبْتَا
english: And if he rides well-groomed, adorned steeds, then you have ridden the paths of piety.

arabic: وَمَهْمَا افْتَضَّ أَبْكَارَ الغَوَانِي  * فَكَمْ بِكْرٍ مِنَ الْحِكَمِ افْتَضَضْتَا
english: And no matter how many beautiful virgins he deflowers, how many virgins of wisdom have you deflowered?

arabic: وَلَيْسَ يَضُرُّكَ الإِقْتَارُ شَيْئًا  * إِذَا مَا أَنْتَ رَبَّكَ قَدْ عَرَفْتَا
english: And poverty will not harm you in the least, so long as you have truly known your Lord.

arabic: فَمَاذَا عِندَهُ لَكَ مِن جَمِيلٍ  * إِذَا بِفِنَاءِ طَاعَتِهِ أَنَخْتَا
english: Then what beautiful reward does He have in store for you, if you have set yourself down at the doorstep of His obedience?

arabic: فَقَابِلْ بِالْقَبُولِ لِنُصْحِ قَوْلِي  * فَإِنْ أَعْرَضْتَ عَنْهُ فَقَدْ خَسِرْتَا
english: So accept my words of advice with acceptance, for if you turn away from it, then surely you have lost.

arabic: وَإِنْ رَاعَيْتَهُ قَوْلًا وَفِعْلًا  * وَتَاجَرْتَ الإِلَهَ بِهِ رَبِحْتَا
english: And if you observe it in both speech and action, and trade with Allah through it — you will profit.

arabic: فَلَيسَتْ هَذِهِ الدُّنْيَا بِشَيْءٍ  * تَسُوؤُكَ حِقْبَةً وَتَسُرُّ وَقْتًا
english: This world is nothing of real worth, it harms you for a long while and pleases you for a moment.

arabic: وَغَايَتُهَا إِذَا فَكَّرَتْ فِيهَا  * كَفَيْئِكَ أَوْ كَحُلْمِكَ إِذْ حَلَمْتَا
english: And its end, if you reflect on it, it is like your shadow, or like a dream you dreamt.

arabic: سُجِنْتَ بِهَا وَأَنْتَ لَهَا مُحِبٌّ  * فَكَيْفَ تُحِبُّ مَا فِيهِ سُجِنْتَا
english: You have been imprisoned in it, while you love it, so how can you love that in which you are imprisoned?

arabic: وَتُطْعِمُكَ الطَّعَامَ وَعَنْ قَرِيبٍ  * سَتَطْعَمُ مِنْكَ مَا فِيهَا طَعِمْتَا
english: It feeds you food, and soon, it will consume you, just as you once consumed from it.

arabic: وَتَعْرَى إِنْ لَبِسْتَ لَهَا ثِيَابًا  * وَتُكْسَى إِنْ مَلابِسَهَا خَلَعْتَا
english: You become naked if you wear clothes for it, and you are clothed if you take off its garments.

arabic: وَتَشْهَدُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ دَفْنَ خِلٍّ  * كَأَنَّكَ لَا تُرَادُ لِمَا شَهِدْتَا
english: Every day you witness the burial of a close friend, as though you were not meant for what you have witnessed.

arabic: وَلَمْ تُخْلَقْ لِتَعْمُرَهَا وَلَكِنْ  * لِتَعْبُرَهَا فَجِدَّ لِمَا خُلِقْتَا
english: You were not created to build up (this world), but to pass through it, so strive earnestly for what you were created for.

arabic: وَإِنْ هُدِمَتْ فَزِدْهَا أَنْتَ هَدْمًا  * وَحَصِّنْ أَمْرَ دِينِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتَا
english: And if (this world) is being destroyed, then add to its destruction yourself, and fortify the matter of your religion as much as you can.

arabic: وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَى مَا فَاتَ مِنْهَا  * إِذَا مَا أَنتَ فِي أُخْرَاكَ فُزْتَا
english: And do not grieve over what you missed from this world, if you have triumphed in your Hereafter.

arabic: فَلَيسَ بِنافِعٍ مَا نِلْتَ مِنْهَا  * مِنَ الْفَانِي إِذَا الْبَاقِي حُرِمْتَا
english: What you gain of fleeting things from it is of no benefit, if you are deprived of the everlasting [the hereafter].

arabic: وَلا تَضْحَكْ مَعَ السُّفَهَاءِ يَوْمًا  * فَإِنَّكَ سَوفَ تَبْكِي إِن ضَحِكْتَا
english: Do not laugh with the foolish for even a day, for if you laugh (with them), you will surely weep [in the hereafter].

arabic: وَمَنْ لَكَ بِالسُرُورِ وَأَنتَ رَهْنٌ  * وَمَا تَدْرِي أَتُفْدَى أَمْ غُلِلْتَا
english: And who can give you joy while you are a captive, and you don’t know — will you be ransomed or held in chains?

arabic: وَسَلْ مِن رَبِّكَ التَّوْفِيقَ فِيهَا  * وَأَخْلِصْ فِي السُّؤَالِ إِذَا سَأَلْتَا
english: Ask your Lord for success (tawfiq) in it (i.e., in life or religion), and be sincere in your asking when you ask.

arabic: وَنَادِ إِذَا سَجَدْتَ لَهُ اعْتِرَافًا  * بِمَا نَادَاهُ ذُو النُّونِ بْنُ مَتَّى
english: And call upon Him in prostration with confession, with the same words that Dhu Al-Nun, son of Matta, called with.

arabic: وَلَازِمْ بَابَهُ قَرْعًا عَسَاهُ  * سَيفْتَحُ بَابَهُ لَكَ إِنْ قَرَعْتَا
english: And persist in knocking at His door — perhaps He will open His door for you if you knock.

arabic: وَأَكْثِرْ ذِكْرَهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ دَأْبًا  * لِتُذْكَرَ فِي السَّمَاءِ إِذَا ذَكَرْتَا
english: Increase His remembrance constantly on earth, so that you may be mentioned in the heavens when you remember Him.

arabic: وَلَا تَقُلْ الصِّبَا فِيهِ امْتِهَالٌ  * وَفَكِّرْ كَمْ صَغِيرٍ قَدْ دَفَنْتَا
english: And do not say, “Youth allows delay”, but reflect — how many young ones have you buried?

arabic: وَقُلْ يَا نَاصِحِي بَلْ أَنْتَ أَوْلَى  * بِنُصْحِكَ لَوْ بِعَقْلِكَ قَدْ نَظَرْتَا
english: And say: “Oh my adviser, rather you are more deserving of your own advice — if only you had reflected with your mind.”

arabic: تُقَطِّعُنِي عَلَى التَّفْرِيطِ لَوْمًا  * وَبِالتَّفْرِيطِ دَهْرَكَ قَدْ قَطَعْتَا
english: You tear me apart with blame for my negligence, while you’ve spent your whole life in negligence yourself.

arabic: وَفِي صِغَرِي تُخَوِّفُنِي الْمَنَايَا  * وَمَا تَدْرِي بِحَالِكَ حَيْثُ شِخْتَا
english: In my youth, you used to frighten me with death, yet you do not even know your own state now that you’ve grown old.

arabic: وَكُنْتَ مَعَ الصِّبَا أَهْدَى سَبِيلًا  * فَما لَكَ بَعْدَ شَيْبِكَ قَدْ نَكَثْتَا
english: You were more rightly guided in your youth, so what happened to you — now that you’re grey-haired — that you’ve broken (your path)?

arabic: وَهَا أَنَا لَمْ أَخُضْ بَحْرَ الْخَطَايَا  * كَما قَدْ خُضْتَهُ حَتَّى غَرِقْتَا
english: And here I am — I did not plunge into the sea of sins, as you have plunged until you drowned.

arabic: وَلَمْ أَشْرَبْ حُمَيَّا أُمِّ دَفْرٍ  * وَأَنتَ شَرِبْتَهَا حَتَّى سَكِرْتَا
english: I never drank the bitter wine of this foul life, while you drank it until you became drunk.

arabic: وَلَمْ أَنْشَأْ بِعَصْرٍ فِيهِ نَفْعٌ  * وَأَنْتَ نَشَأْتَ فِيهِ وَمَا انْتَفَعْتَا
english: I was not raised in a time where there was benefit. while you were — but did not benefit.

arabic: وَلَمْ أَحْلُلْ بِوَادٍ فِيهِ ظُلْمٌ  * وَأَنتَ حَلَلْتَ فِيهِ وَانْتَهَكْتَا
english: I never settled in a valley where there was injustice, but you settled there — and violated (its sanctity).

arabic: لَقَدْ صَاحَبْتَ أَعْلَامًا كِبَارًا  * وَلَمْ أَرَكَ اقْتَدَيْتَ بِمَنْ صَحِبْتَا
english: Indeed, you accompanied great scholars, yet I never saw you follow those you accompanied.

arabic: وَنَادَاكَ الْكِتَابُ فَلَمْ تُجِبْهُ  * وَنَبَّهَكَ الْمَشِيبُ فَمَا انتَبَهْتَا
english: The Book called out to you, but you did not respond, and grey hair alerted you — but you did not awaken.

arabic: وَيَقْبُحُ بِالفَتَى فِعْلُ التَّصَابِي  * وَأَقْبَحُ مِنْهُ شَيْخٌ قَد تَفَتَّى
english: Childish behaviour is unbecoming of a young man, but even uglier is an old man acting like a youth.

arabic: وَنَفْسَكَ ذُمَّ لَا تَذُمُمْ سِوَاهَا  * بِعَيْبٍ فَهِيَ أَجْدَرُ مَنْ ذَمَمْتَا
english: Blame your own soul — do not blame others, for of all the flawed, it is most worthy of your blame.

arabic: وَأَنتَ أَحَقُّ بِالتَّفْنِيدِ مِنِّي  * وَلَوْ كُنْتَ اللَّبِيبَ لَمَا نَطَقْتَا
english: You are more deserving of criticism than I am, and if you were truly wise, you would not have spoken.

arabic: فَلَوْ بَكَتِ الدِّمَا عَيْنَاكَ خَوْفًا  * لِذَنْبِكَ لَمْ أَقُلْ لَكَ قَدْ أَمِنْتَا
english: Even if your eyes were to weep blood out of fear for your sin, I still would not say to you: “You are safe.”

arabic: وَمَنْ لَكَ بِالأَمانِ وَأَنْتَ عَبْدٌ  * أُمِرْتَ فَمَا ائْتَمَرْتَ وَلَا أَطَعْتَا
english: And who can grant you safety while you are a slave, who was commanded — yet neither acted nor obeyed?

arabic: ثَقُلْتَ مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ وَلَسْتَ تَخْشَى  * لِجَهْلِكَ أَنْ تَخِفَّ إِذَا وُزِنْتَا
english: You’ve become heavy with sins — yet you don’t fear that, out of ignorance, you might be found light when weighed (on the scales).

arabic: وَتُشْفِقُ لِلْمُصِرِّ عَلَى الْمَعَاصِي  * وَتَرْحَمُهُ وَنَفْسَكَ مَا رَحِمْتَا
english: You feel compassion for the one who persists in sin, you show him mercy — yet your own soul you have not shown mercy to.

arabic: رَجَعْتَ القَهْقَرَى وَخَبَطْتَ عَشْوًا  * لَعَمْرُكَ لَوْ وَصَلْتَ لَمَا رَجَعْتَا
english: You turned backward and stumbled blindly, by your life! If you had truly reached [the goal], you wouldn’t have turned back.

arabic: وَلَوْ وَافَيْتَ رَبَّكَ دُونَ ذَنْبٍ  * وَنُوقِشْتَ الحِسَابَ إِذًا هَلَكْتَا
english: Even if you met your Lord with no sin, if you were reckoned in detail, you would perish.

arabic: وَلَمْ يَظْلِمْكَ فِي عَمَلٍ وَلَكِنْ  * عَسِيرٌ أَنْ تَقُومَ بِمَا حَمَلْتَا
english: He has not wronged you in any deed, but it’s difficult for you to stand with what you’ve carried.

arabic: وَلَوْ قَدْ جِئْتَ يَوْمَ الحَشْرِفَرْدًا  * وَأَبْصَرْتَ المَنَازِلَ فِيهِ شَتَّى
english: And if you were to come on the Day of Gathering alone, and saw the ranks (of people) there scattered.

arabic: لَأَعْظَمْتَ النَّدَامَةَ فِيهِ لَهْفًا  * عَلى مَا فِي حَياتِكَ قَدْ أَضَعْتَا
english: You will feel great regret and anguish in that moment over what you had wasted of your life.

arabic: تَفِرُّ مِنَ الهَجِيرِ وَتَتَّقِيْهِ  * فَهَلَّا مِنْ جَهَنَّمَ قَدْ فَرَرْتَا
english: You flee from the midday heat and protect yourself from it, so why haven’t you fled from Hell (Jahannam)?

arabic: وَلَسْتَ تُطِيْقُ أَهْوَنَهَا عَذَابًا  * وَلَوْ كُنْتَ الْحَدِيدَ بِهَا لَذُبْتَا
english: You are unable to endure the lightest of its punishments, and even if you were iron, you would melt in it.

arabic: وَلَا تُنْكِرْ فَإِنَّ الأَمْرَ جِدٌّ  * وَلَيسَ كَمَا حَسِبْتَا وَلَا ظَنَنْتَا
english: And do not deny (it), for the matter is serious, it is not as you assumed or thought.

arabic: أَبَا بَكْرٍ كَشَفْتَ أَقَلَّ عَيْبِي  * وَأَكْثَرَهُ وَمُعْظَمَهُ سَتَرْتَا
english: Oh Abu Bakr, you uncovered the least of my faults, and most of them — the greatest of them — you concealed.

arabic: فَقُلْ مَا شِئْتَ فِيَّ مِنَ الْمَخَازِي  * وَضَاعِفْهَا فَإِنَّكَ قَدْ صَدَقْتَا
english: So say whatever you wish about my disgraces, and double them — for you have spoken the truth.

arabic: وَمَهْمَا عِبْتَنِي فَلِفَرْطِ عِلْمِي  * بِبَاطِنَتِي كَأَنَّكَ قَد مَدَحْتَا
english: And no matter how much you criticise me, because of my deep knowledge of my inner self, it’s as if you praised me.

arabic: فَلَا تَرْضَ المَعايِبَ فَهْيَ عَارٌ  * عَظِيمٌ يُورِثُ المَحْبُوبَ مَقْتًا
english: So do not be content with disgraceful traits, for they are a great shame, a grave dishonour that brings hatred even to the beloved.

arabic: وَيَهْوِي بِالْوَجِيهِ مِنَ الثُّرَيَّا  * وَيُبْدِلُهُ مَكَانَ الْفَوْقِ تَحْتًا
english: And it (disgrace) brings down the noble person from the Pleiades (the highest stars), and replaces his high position with one below.

arabic: كَمَا الطَّاعَاتُ تُبْدِلُكَ الدَّرَارِي  * وَتَجْعَلُكَ الْقَرِيبَ وَإِنْ بَعُدْتَا
english: Just as acts of obedience elevate you to the stars, and make you near [to Allah], even if you were far.

arabic: وَتَنْشُرُ عَنْكَ فِي الدُّنْيَا جَمِيلًا  * وَتَلْقَى الْبِرَّ فِيهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتَا
english: And it (obedience) spreads a beautiful reputation for you in the world, and you encounter righteousness in it wherever you go.

arabic: وَتَمْشِي فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا عَزِيزًا  * وَتَجْنِي الْحَمْدَ فِيمَا قَدْ غَرَسْتَا
english: And you walk through its (the world’s) pathways in honour, and you reap praise from what you have planted.

arabic: وَأَنْتَ الْآنَ لَمْ تُعْرَفْ بِعَيْبٍ  * وَلَا دَنَّسْتَ ثَوْبَكَ مُذْ نَشَأْتَا
english: And until now, you have not been known for any fault, nor have you stained your garment (i.e. reputation) since you were born.

arabic: وَلَا سَابَقْتَ فِي مَيْدَانِ زُورٍ  * وَلَا أَوْضَعْتَ فِيهِ وَلَا خَبَبْتَا
english: And you have never raced in the field of falsehood, nor have you sped within it, nor swindled in it.

arabic: فَإِنْ لَمْ تَنْأَ عَنْهُ نَشِبْتَ فِيهِ  * وَمَنْ لَكَ بِالخَلَاصِ إِذَا نَشِبْتَا
english: If you do not distance yourself from it, you will become entangled in it, and who can deliver you once you’re entangled?

arabic: تُدَنِّسُ مَا تَطَهَّرَ مِنْكَ حَتَّى  * كَأَنَّكَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ مَا طَهُرْتَا
english: You defile what had been purified from you, until it is as if you had never been pure at all.

arabic: وَصِرْتَ أَسِيرَ ذَنْبِكَ فِي وَثَاقٍ  * وَكَيْفَ لَكَ الفِكَاكُ وَقَدْ أُسِرْتَا
english: And you have become a prisoner of your sin, bound in chains, so how can you escape, when you’ve already been captured?

arabic: فَخِفْ أَبْنَاءَ جِنْسِكَ وَاخْشَ مِنْهُمْ  * كَمَا تَخْشَى الضَّرَاغِمَ وَالسَّبَنْتَى
english: So fear the sons of your own kind (i.e. people) and be wary of them, just as you would fear lions and savage beasts.

arabic: وَخَالِطْهُمْ وَزَايِلْهُمْ حِذَارًا  * وَكُنْ كَالْسَّامِرِيِّ إِذَا لُمِسْتَا
english: Mix with them, but separate from them cautiously, and be like As-Samiri when you are touched.

arabic: وَإِنْ جَهِلُوا عَلَيْكَ فَقُلْ سَلَامٌ  * لَعَلَّكَ سَوْفَ تَسْلَمُ إِنْ فَعَلْتَا
english: And if they behave ignorantly toward you, then say: ‘Peace’, perhaps you will be safe if you do so.

arabic: وَمَن لَكَ بِالسَّلَامَةِ فِي زَمَانٍ  * تَنَالُ العِصْمَ إِلَّا إِنْ عُصِمْتَا
english: And who can guarantee you safety in a time where protection is only attained if you are [already] protected?

arabic: وَلَا تَلْبَثْ بِحَيٍّ فِيهِ ضَيْمٌ  * يُمِيتُ الْقَلْبَ إِلَّا إِنْ كُبِلْتَا
english: Do not remain in a community (or among people) where there is injustice, for it kills the heart — unless you are bound (i.e. have no choice).

arabic: وَغَرِّبْ فَالتَّغَرُّّبُ فِيهِ خَيْرٌ  * وَشَرِّقْ إِنْ بِرِيقِكَ قَدْ شَرِقْتَا
english: So go west, for in exile, there can be goodness, and go east, if you’ve choked on your own spit.

arabic: فَلَيْسَ الزُّهْدُ فِي الدُّنْيَا خُمُولًا  * لَأَنْتَ بِهَا الأَمِيرُ إِذَا زَهِدتَّا
english: Renouncing the world is not sluggishness, in fact, you are its leader if you renounce it.

arabic: وَلَوْ فَوْقَ الأَمِيرِ تَكُونُ فِيهَا  * سُمُوًّا وَارْتِفَاعًا كُنْتَ أَنتَا
english: And even if there were someone above the prince in status within it in loftiness and elevation — that would be you.

arabic: وَإِنْ فَارَقْتَهَا وَخَرَجْتَ مِنْهَا  * إِلَى دَارِ السَّلَامِ فَقَدْ سَلِمْتَا
english: And if you part from it (the world) and depart from it, to the Abode of Peace — then truly, you will be saved.

arabic: وَإِنْ أَكْرَمْتَهَا وَنَظَرْتَ فِيهَا  * بِإِكْرَامٍ فَنَفْسَكَ قَدْ أَهَنْتَا
english: And if you honour it (the world) and look upon it with esteem, then it is your own soul that you have disgraced.

arabic: جَمَعْتُ لَكَ النَّصَائِحَ فَامْتَثِلْهَا  * حَياتَكَ فَهِيَ أَفضَلُ مَا امْتَثَلْتَا
english: I have gathered for you the advice — so follow it, in your life, for it is the best thing you could ever follow.

arabic: وَطَوَّلْتُ الْعِتَابَ وَزِدْتُ فِيهِ  * لِأَنَّكَ فِي الْبَطَالَةِ قَدْ أَطَلْتَا
english: And I lengthened the reproach and increased it, because you have prolonged your time in idleness.

arabic: وَلَا يَغْرُرْكَ تَقْصِيرِي وَسَهْوِي  * وَخُذْ بِوَصِيَّتِي لَكَ إِنْ رَشَدتَّا
english: And let not my shortcomings or forgetfulness deceive you, take my advice to heart, if you are rightly guided.

arabic: وَقَدْ أَردَفْتُهَا تِسْعًا حِسَانًا  * وَكَانَتْ قَبْلَ ذَا مِئَةً وَسِتًّا
english: And I followed it up with nine beautiful ones, and before that, they were a hundred and six.

arabic: وَصَلِّ عَلَى تَمَامِ الرُّسْلِ رَبِّي  * وَعِتْرَتِهِ الكَرِيمَةِ مَا ذُكِرْتَا
english: And send Salawat on the seal of the prophets of my Lord, and upon his noble family, whenever you are reminded.
